member.name: T:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.BoundedObservableCollection`1
member.summary: An observable collection optimized for prepending items with a fixed maximum size. Uses an internal deque pattern to achieve O(1) prepend operations instead of O(n) Insert(0). Thread-safe for single-threaded UI operations with proper change notifications.
member.typeparam: The type of elements in the collection.

member.name: E:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.BoundedObservableCollection`1.CollectionChanged
member.summary: Occurs when the collection changes.

member.name: E:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.BoundedObservableCollection`1.PropertyChanged
member.summary: Occurs when a property value changes.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.BoundedObservableCollection`1.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a new bounded observable collection with the specified maximum capacity.
member.param: The maximum number of items the collection can hold.

member.name: P:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.BoundedObservableCollection`1.Count
member.summary: Gets the number of elements in the collection.

member.name: P:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.BoundedObservableCollection`1.MaxCapacity
member.summary: Gets the maximum capacity of the collection.

member.name: P:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.BoundedObservableCollection`1.IsReadOnly
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the collection is read-only.

member.name: P:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.BoundedObservableCollection`1.Item(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets or sets the element at the specified index.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.BoundedObservableCollection`1.Prepend(`0)
member.summary: Prepends an item to the beginning of the collection. If the collection is at capacity, the oldest item (at the end) is automatically removed. This is an O(1) amortized operation due to efficient internal management.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.BoundedObservableCollection`1.PrependRange(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{`0})
member.summary: Prepends multiple items to the beginning of the collection efficiently. Items are added in order, so the first item in the enumerable will be at index 0.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.BoundedObservableCollection`1.Add(`0)
member.summary: Adds an item to the end of the collection. If the collection is at capacity, the oldest item (at index 0) is automatically removed.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.BoundedObservableCollection`1.Clear
member.summary: Removes all items from the collection.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.BoundedObservableCollection`1.Contains(`0)
member.summary: Determines whether the collection contains a specific item.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.BoundedObservableCollection`1.CopyTo(`0[],System.Int32)
member.summary: Copies the elements of the collection to an array.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.BoundedObservableCollection`1.GetEnumerator
member.summary: Returns an enumerator that iterates through the collection.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.BoundedObservableCollection`1.IndexOf(`0)
member.summary: Searches for the specified item and returns its index.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.BoundedObservableCollection`1.Insert(System.Int32,`0)
member.summary: Inserts an item at the specified index.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.BoundedObservableCollection`1.Remove(`0)
member.summary: Removes the first occurrence of a specific item from the collection.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.BoundedObservableCollection`1.RemoveAt(System.Int32)
member.summary: Removes the item at the specified index.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.BoundedObservableCollection`1.System#Collections#IEnumerable#GetEnumerator
member.summary: Returns an enumerator that iterates through the collection.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.BoundedObservableCollection`1.ReplaceAll(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{`0})
member.summary: Replaces the contents of this collection with items from another enumerable.

member.summary: Creates a snapshot of the current items as a list.

member.typeparam: The type of elements in the buffer.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBuffer`1.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new circular buffer with the specified capacity.
member.param: The maximum number of elements the buffer can hold.
member.exception: Thrown when capacity is less than 1.

member.name: P:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBuffer`1.Capacity
member.summary: Gets the maximum capacity of the buffer.

member.name: P:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBuffer`1.Count
member.summary: Gets the current number of elements in the buffer.

member.name: P:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBuffer`1.IsEmpty
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the buffer is empty.

member.name: P:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBuffer`1.IsFull
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the buffer is full.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBuffer`1.Add(`0)
member.summary: Adds an element to the buffer. If the buffer is full, the oldest element is overwritten.

member.name: P:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBuffer`1.Item(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets the element at the specified index, where index 0 is the oldest element and index Count-1 is the newest element.
member.param: The zero-based index of the element to get.
member.returns: The element at the specified index.
member.exception: Thrown when index is out of range.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBuffer`1.GetNewest
member.summary: Gets the most recently added element.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBuffer`1.GetOldest
member.summary: Gets the oldest element in the buffer.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBuffer`1.TryGetNewest(`0@)
member.summary: Tries to get the most recently added element.
member.param: When this method returns, contains the newest element if successful.
member.returns: True if the buffer is not empty; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBuffer`1.TryGetFromNewest(System.Int32,`0@)
member.summary: Tries to get the element at the specified offset from the newest element. Offset 0 returns the newest, offset 1 returns the second newest, etc.
member.param: Offset from the newest element (0 = newest).
When this method returns, contains the element if successful; otherwise, the default value for the type.
member.returns: True if the element exists at the specified offset; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBuffer`1.Clear
member.summary: Clears all elements from the buffer.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBuffer`1.ToArray
member.summary: Copies all elements to an array, ordered from oldest to newest.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBuffer`1.GetEnumerator
member.summary: Returns an enumerator that iterates through the buffer from oldest to newest.

member.name: T:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBufferExtensions
member.summary: Extension methods for CircularBuffer with numeric types.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBufferExtensions.Sum(Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBuffer{System.Double})
member.summary: Calculates the sum of all elements in the buffer.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBufferExtensions.Average(Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBuffer{System.Double})
member.summary: Calculates the average of all elements in the buffer.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBufferExtensions.Max(Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBuffer{System.Double})
member.summary: Finds the maximum value in the buffer.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBufferExtensions.Min(Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBuffer{System.Double})
member.summary: Finds the minimum value in the buffer.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBufferExtensions.CalculateRate(Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBuffer{System.Double},System.Double)
member.summary: Calculates the rate of change (delta) between the two most recent values.
member.param: The buffer to calculate rate for.
The time interval between samples in seconds.
member.returns: The rate of change per second, or 0 if insufficient data.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBufferExtensions.CalculatePercentageChange(Meridian.Ui.Services.Collections.CircularBuffer{System.Double},System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Calculates the percentage change between two values at specified offsets from the newest element.
member.param: The buffer to calculate percentage change for.
Offset of the base value from newest (1 = second newest).
Offset of the comparison value from newest (0 = newest).
member.returns: The percentage change, or null if division by zero or insufficient data.
member.remarks: Formula: ((toValue - fromValue) / fromValue) * 100 Returns null if fromValue is zero to avoid division by zero.

member.name: T:Meridian.Ui.Services.Services.FixtureModeDetector
member.summary: Detects and tracks whether the application is running in fixture/offline mode. Provides a centralized check so all UI pages can show a hard visual distinction when viewing sample data instead of live backend data.
member.remarks: Fixture mode is activated by: - Command-line flag: --fixture - Environment variable: MDC_FIXTURE_MODE=1 - Backend unreachable (auto-detected, triggers offline mode banner)

member.name: T:Meridian.Ui.Services.Services.FixtureScenario
member.summary: Named fixture scenarios for UI development and debugging. Each scenario corresponds to a distinct system state that can be activated at runtime without restarting the application, enabling rapid iteration over all visual states in a single session.

member.name: F:Meridian.Ui.Services.Services.FixtureScenario.Connected
member.summary: System fully connected with healthy metrics — the happy-path state. Providers are active, throughput is nominal, no errors.

member.name: F:Meridian.Ui.Services.Services.FixtureScenario.Disconnected
member.summary: System disconnected from all providers. Useful for testing empty-state / reconnection UI paths.

member.name: F:Meridian.Ui.Services.Services.FixtureScenario.Degraded
member.summary: System partially connected with degraded metrics. Some providers are unavailable or rate-limited; throughput is reduced.

member.name: F:Meridian.Ui.Services.Services.FixtureScenario.Error
member.summary: System in error state — provider errors, high drop rate, low throughput. Useful for testing error banners, alerts, and recovery UI flows.

member.name: F:Meridian.Ui.Services.Services.FixtureScenario.Loading
member.summary: System is initializing or loading — connections pending, data not yet available. Useful for testing skeleton/loading states and progress indicators.

member.name: T:Meridian.Ui.Services.DesktopJsonOptions
member.summary:
Centralized JSON serialization options for desktop applications (WPF). Consolidates all JsonSerializerOptions to avoid duplication across services.

member.name: F:Meridian.Ui.Services.DesktopJsonOptions.Compact
member.summary: Options for compact JSON output (storage, API communication). - No indentation - CamelCase property naming - Null values omitted - Case-insensitive property matching on read

member.name: F:Meridian.Ui.Services.DesktopJsonOptions.PrettyPrint
member.summary: Options for pretty-printed JSON output (config files, debugging, user-visible exports). - Indented output for readability - CamelCase property naming - Null values omitted - Case-insensitive property matching on read

member.name: F:Meridian.Ui.Services.DesktopJsonOptions.Api
member.summary: Options for API communication (matches most REST APIs). - No indentation (compact wire format) - CamelCase property naming - Case-insensitive property matching on read

member.name: T:Meridian.Ui.Services.ErrorMessages
member.summary:
Centralized error message templates with actionable remediation suggestions.
All error messages should include: 1. What happened (the error) 2. Why it might have happened (context) 3. What the user can do about it (remedy)
This approach reduces support burden and improves user experience.

member.name: T:Meridian.Ui.Services.HttpClientConfiguration
member.summary: Extension methods for configuring HttpClient instances via IHttpClientFactory in desktop apps.
member.remarks:
Implements TD-10: Replace instance HttpClient with IHttpClientFactory. Benefits: - Proper connection pooling and DNS refresh - Prevents socket exhaustion - Centralized configuration for timeouts, headers, retry policies - Better testability through DI
This intentionally duplicates resilience policies from Meridian.Infrastructure.Http.SharedResiliencePolicies because the WPF desktop app cannot reference the Infrastructure project (XAML compiler limitation). When updating retry/circuit breaker policies, keep both implementations in sync.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.HttpClientConfiguration.AddDesktopHttpClients(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection)
member.summary: Registers all named HttpClient configurations with the DI container for desktop apps.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.HttpClientConfiguration.AddStandardResiliencePolicy(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IHttpClientBuilder)
member.summary: Adds standard resilience policies (retry with exponential backoff, circuit breaker).

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.HttpClientConfiguration.GetRetryPolicy
member.summary: Creates a retry policy with exponential backoff for transient HTTP errors.

member.name: M:Meridian.Ui.Services.HttpClientConfiguration.GetCircuitBreakerPolicy
member.summary: Creates a circuit breaker policy to prevent cascading failures.

member.name: T:Meridian.Ui.Services.HttpClientFactoryProvider
member.summary: Static factory for creating HttpClient instances from IHttpClientFactory in desktop apps. Provides backward-compatible static access for services not yet fully converted to DI.
member.remarks: This is a transitional pattern. New code should inject IHttpClientFactory directly.

member.summary: Gets an HttpClient for the specified named client with header configuration.

member.summary: Checks if the factory has been initialized.

member.name: T:Meridian.Ui.Services.StorageOptimizationAdvisorService
member.summary:
Service for analyzing storage and providing optimization recommendations. Implements Feature Refinement #63 - Archive Storage Optimization Advisor.
This service provides storage analysis and delegates operations to the core service via HTTP API endpoints when available: - /api/storage/catalog - Storage catalog for file discovery - /api/storage/tiers/statistics - Tier statistics - /api/storage/tiers/plan - Migration planning - /api/storage/tiers/migrate - Tier migration execution - /api/storage/maintenance/defrag - Defragmentation execution
